relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Humans know where to mine silver and refine gold.
Iron and copper are drawn from dust and stone.
Human searching pushes into darkness and shadow.
Shafts are opened where ordinary feet never go.
The earth gives bread above while hidden fire overturns below.
Gem and gold are found in the depths.
No bird of prey knows that hidden path.
No proud beast or lion walks there.
Human hands overturn rock and root of mountain.
Eyes search the stone and find precious things.
Hidden things are brought to light.
But wisdom is not found by the same search.
Mortals do not know its true value, nor is it found as a living-land treasure.
The Deep and the Sea both say wisdom is not in them.
Gold and silver cannot buy it.
Ophir’s gold and precious stones cannot price it.
Gold, glass, and fine vessels cannot equal it.
Coral, crystal, and pearls fall beneath wisdom’s worth.
Even Cush’s topaz cannot match it.
Again the question returns: where does wisdom come from?
It is hidden from living eyes and winged sight.
Abaddon and Death have only heard a rumor of it.
The Divine alone understands its way and place.
The Divine sees the ends of earth and all beneath heaven.
Wind and waters were weighed and measured.
Rain and thunder were given their paths.
Then wisdom was seen, established, and searched out by the Divine.
For humans, wisdom begins in reverent alignment and turning away from harm.
